description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a rich brown finish and a metallic head. The axe head is forged from steel, with a broad, single-edged blade that curves outward and a flat poll on the opposite side. The blade has a pronounced beard, extending downward for increased cutting surface and control. The handle appears ergonomically shaped, tapering at the grip for comfort and control, and is securely fitted into the axe head. The metal head is matte gray, showing no visible decorative engravings or branding. This tool is typical for chopping wood or similar tasks and would be found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance areas.
description: A hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, bearded steel blade, designed for chopping tasks. The handle is brown and ergonomic, while the blade is matte gray and robust.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from an angled perspective, highlighting the curvature of the wooden handle and the full profile of the steel blade.
description_view_1: A side view showing the entire length of the handle and the full cutting edge of the blade, emphasizing the axe's slender profile.
description_view_2: A front-facing view of the blade, displaying the width and curvature of the cutting edge and the thickness of the axe head.
description_view_3: The opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with the handle and blade profile visible, showing the poll and the attachment point.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with an ergonomic grip, oval cross-section, and smooth finish.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, broad steel blade with a pronounced beard and a flat poll; no visible decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle fitted through the axe head.
